summary: Response times worsened for two distinct reasons in successive years: in FY24, DEP pointed to a small number of anonymous chemical-odor complaints that lacked enough location or callback detail to verify quickly, and in FY25 to a rise in complaints tied to damaged lithium-ion batteries combined with a staffing shortage in DEP's hazardous materials unit. DEP has since rolled out a new triage system with other City agencies that it credits with cutting response times substantially in early FY26.

sources.label: DEP, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing for a couple of distinct reasons; a new triage system has since cut response times
